user_query:
Build a small, production-patterned Rust async web service skeleton based on my spec and document the design choices.

Requirements:
1) Read input/spec.json for the service_name and input/endpoints.csv for the endpoints you should scaffold.
2) Create these files:
   - output/manifest.json — a machine-readable manifest describing the package (use service_name for the package name) and dependencies you’d include. Include at least: tokio, axum, serde, serde_json, clap, thiserror, anyhow. Use simple fields: {"package_name": string, "edition": "2024", "dependencies": [ ... ]}.
   - output/src_main.rs.md — a single Markdown file containing the full Rust source for src/main.rs. Implement:
     • A Clap-based CLI with #[derive(Parser)] and an enum subcommand including Serve and Check.
     • #[tokio::main(flavor = "multi_thread")] async fn main() -> anyhow::Result<()> {}
     • An Axum Router with GET /healthz and handlers for every endpoint listed in input/endpoints.csv. Include the literal route paths (e.g., "/items", "/items/:id").
     • Shared state via Arc<RwLock<_>> and at least one use of tokio::select! in a realistic place (e.g., graceful shutdown signal vs. server serve future).
     • A custom error type AppError using thiserror (#[derive(Error)]) and error propagation via anyhow in main. Avoid using unwrap anywhere — prefer ? and proper Result handling.
     • Add brief comments where relevant to show ownership/borrowing choices and to note avoiding blocking I/O in async (suggest tokio::fs or spawn_blocking).
   - output/README.md — a human-readable README explaining how to run the CLI (commands and flags) and listing all routes (method + path) derived from input/endpoints.csv. Mention the service_name in the README and include a short usage example.
   - output/DESIGN.md — explain key decisions: thiserror for library error types vs anyhow in the binary, ownership/borrowing mental model for shared state, why Arc<RwLock> (and trade-offs vs channels), an explicit note on avoiding sync I/O in async (tokio::fs or spawn_blocking), and how you would add a database later with SQLx. Include concise code snippets where helpful, but keep this as a prose document.

Conventions:
- Use workspace-relative paths and only write files under output/.
- Ensure every endpoint from input/endpoints.csv is present in both the router code and the README’s route list.
- Keep the code and docs self-contained; do not assume external setup. You don’t need a full Cargo project; just provide the main.rs content in output/src_main.rs.md and the dependency manifest in output/manifest.json.

Please proceed after reading input/spec.json and input/endpoints.csv and write all files under output/ exactly as specified.
